title: DROP and TRUNCATE
content:
### DROP and TRUNCATE Statements

DROP removes entire database objects (tables, views, indexes). TRUNCATE removes all rows from a table while keeping the table structure.

---

### DROP TABLE

Removes the table and its data permanently:

```sql
DROP TABLE employees;

-- Prevent error if table doesn't exist
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS employees;
```

### DROP Other Objects

```sql
DROP VIEW IF EXISTS active_employees;
DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_name;
DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS test_db;
DROP PROCEDURE IF EXISTS get_employees;
```

### TRUNCATE TABLE

Removes all rows from a table (cannot be rolled back in most databases):

```sql
TRUNCATE TABLE temp_data;
```

### DELETE vs TRUNCATE vs DROP

| Operation | Removes | Structure | Can Rollback | Speed | Reset Identity |
|-----------|---------|-----------|-------------|-------|----------------|
| DELETE | Selected rows | Kept | Yes (in transaction) | Slow | No |
| TRUNCATE | All rows | Kept | No (usually) | Fast | Yes |
| DROP | All rows + structure | Removed | No | Fastest | N/A |

### TRUNCATE Limitations

```sql
-- Cannot truncate if foreign key references exist
TRUNCATE TABLE departments;  -- Error if employees reference it

-- Must disable constraints first or use DELETE
DELETE FROM departments;  -- Works but slower
```

### DROP DATABASE

```sql
DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS old_database;
```

### DROP COLUMN (via ALTER TABLE)

```sql
ALTER TABLE employees DROP COLUMN temporary_column;
```

> **Key Point:** DROP removes the entire table, TRUNCATE empties it, DELETE removes specific rows. TRUNCATE is faster than DELETE but offers less control and rollback capability.

quiz.question: What is the key difference between DELETE and TRUNCATE?
options: TRUNCATE removes all rows and is faster, DELETE can filter rows
TRUNCATE can be used with WHERE

quiz.question: Does DROP TABLE keep the table structure?
options: Only if IF EXISTS is used
The structure is moved to temp
